title: Tuples 📦
description: Group exactly N different values together.

slides.title: Compounding It! 🧱
slides.content:
**Compound types** can group multiple values into one type.

Rust has two main primitive compound types: **tuples** and **arrays**.

slides.title: The Tuple 📦
slides.content:
A **tuple** is a way of bundling together a group of values. But the values can be of **different types**.

```rust
let tup = (500, 6.4, 1);
```

slides.title: Tuple Types 📝

quizData.question: Can a Tuple contain elements of different data types (e.g., a number and a boolean)?

options.text: No, all elements must match exactly.

quizData.explanation: Tuples are explicitly designed to bundle together values with different, specific types.

slides.title: Accessing Tuples 🕵️
Dot Notation 🎯
slides.content:
Alternatively, you can access a specific tuple element directly by using a period `.` followed by its 0-based index.

```rust
let my_tup = (500, 6.4, 1);
let five_hundred = my_tup.0; // Index 0
let one = my_tup.2; // Index 2
```

quizData.question: Given `let t = ('x', 10, true);`, how do you access the `10`?
quizData.explanation: Tuples use dot notation `.1` rather than array brackets `[1]` to access the element at the 1st index (which is `10`).
